## TASK

You are given a Python source file that has been processed by an identifier-renaming and whitespace-substitution scheme.  Inputs:

- The scrambled bytes of the source (section below).
- A pointer at the public documentation of the scheme.
- A goal statement specifying the answer you must produce.

Tools the evaluation environment provides:

- A `notepad/` directory you may read and write.  Any file you create there persists across calls within this run.  Use it to record partial decodings, hypothesised compound→keyword mappings, candidate answers, intermediate computations — anything you would otherwise need to keep in working memory.  Writing to the notepad is the supported way to think across many tool calls without re-deriving previous work.
- (If this challenge enables it) a shell to run small scripts against the inputs.  The challenge config controls whether shell is available; not every challenge enables it.

When you have an answer, write a single JSON object on a line by itself of the form `{"answer": "..."}`.  Do not include additional explanation in the JSON line; explanation may go in tool calls preceding the final answer.

## OBFUSCATION DOCS

Babbleon v2 layer documentation: `docs/v2/structure-scrambling.md` (whitespace-as-words mechanism), `crates/v2-babbleon-preprocessor/src/python_keywords.rs` (the 35 Python hard keywords scrambled by layer 2), and `crates/v2-babbleon-preprocessor/src/whitespace_wordlist.rs` (HKDF derivation).  Layers applied in this run: layer 2 + layer 2b + layer 3 (Python keywords, Python operators — parentheses, comparison operators, `:`, `=`, brackets — AND whitespace runs all substituted with per-epoch wordlist compounds)

## GOAL

The Python source defines a function `auth(seq)` that runs the characters of `seq` through a finite-state machine and returns True iff the machine ends in the Accept state.  Find any non-empty string `seq` for which `auth(seq)` returns True.  Submit the canonical shortest example: the lexicographically smallest 5-character string that succeeds.
